Discovery: Down 54% from ~$9.50 52-week high to $4.51, with Q1 2026 earnings on May 6 (6 days away) as an imminent binary catalyst — management announced 16% workforce reduction + $500M+ in annualized cost savings targeting profitability inflection, revenue growing +12% YoY with ad engine stabilizing, and the stock sits only 18% above its March 27 low of $3.81; the restructuring + earnings combo is the exact setup that can shift narrative fast at these levels. Hard red flag: CTO sold 2 million shares at/near all-time lows, signaling weak internal conviction — this overrides much of the bull thesis and caps this at SPECULATIVE. Not profitable on GAAP basis yet.
growth_picks.analyst_take: CTO sold 2M shares near multi-year lows = severe conviction flag; $500M cost reset has value but D/E 181.6; May 6 earnings binary; post-earnings entry only if guide holds

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: VLCC charter rates $420K/day (+77% from pre-Hormuz); time charters locked at $93.5K/day floor; up 64% YTD; analysts upgrading on reopening bonanza; 29% institutional ownership is low (T4: 247WallSt, IndexBox, MaritimeNews)

Discovery: Hormuz closure forces all Gulf oil/LNG shipments to reroute via Cape of Good Hope, dramatically extending voyage lengths and absorbing global tanker capacity; Frontline daily charter rates have reached $420K/day on crisis premium; analysts upgrading crude tanker stocks to Buy citing a 'reopening bonanza' as eventual Hormuz normalization will require a massive catch-up surge in tanker utilization; up 64% YTD with additional upside if crisis persists.
growth_picks.analyst_take: VLCC rates $420K/day on Hormuz; time charter floor $93.5K/day protects downside; 64% YTD = priced for perfection; Hormuz reopening = 30%+ instant downside
